subject: Sports
title: Football and National Identity in Brazil
description: A 60-second lesson on Football and National Identity in Brazil.

hook.text: In Brazil, football is not a sport. It's an identity. The yellow jersey is more powerful than the flag.

buildup.text: Brazil has won five World Cups—more than any nation. Football is woven into the country's cultural DNA.

discovery.text: Pelé, Garrincha, Ronaldo, Ronaldinho—Brazilian football produced a style called joga bonito, 'the beautiful game.'

twist.text: When Brazil lost 7-1 to Germany in the 2014 World Cup semifinal at home, the nation mourned like a death in the family.

climax.text: Football gives Brazil a unifying identity that transcends class, race, and politics—a rare thing in any society.

punchline.text: Five World Cups. One identity. In Brazil, football is everything.

quiz.question: How many World Cups has Brazil won?
